title: Robustness, drift and adversarial cases

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Robustness, drift and adversarial cases; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Robustness evaluation defines plausible shifts, malformed inputs, rare conditions, abuse cases, adversarial manipulations, and monitoring triggers, then measures degradation and safe failure rather than one in-distribution score.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A text classifier is stressed with empty, long, misspelled, multilingual, duplicated, and prompt-like inputs, with explicit reject behavior and slice deltas. Your goal is to design and execute a stress-test matrix and specify monitoring triggers. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Supervised learning; Generative AI, LLMs and retrieval; Metrics, thresholds and baselines; Sampling, labels and data quality. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: Robustness evaluation defines plausible shifts, malformed inputs, rare conditions, abuse cases, adversarial manipulations, and monitoring triggers, then measures degradation and safe failure rather than one in-distribution score. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: No finite test proves robustness, adversaries adapt, synthetic perturbations may be unrealistic, drift labels arrive late, monitoring can miss silent harms, and recovery controls need exercises. The learner must reject this overclaim: Passing a standard adversarial test suite certifies robustness against future shifts and attacks.

includes: Evaluate distribution shifts, malformed inputs, rare cases, abuse and changing performance after deployment.
Design and execute a stress-test matrix and specify monitoring triggers.
excludes: No finite test proves robustness, adversaries adapt, synthetic perturbations may be unrealistic, drift labels arrive late, monitoring can miss silent harms, and recovery controls need exercises.

outcomes: Design and execute a stress-test matrix and specify monitoring triggers.

examples.title: Robustness, drift and adversarial cases: bounded direct case

walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: A text classifier is stressed with empty, long, misspelled, multilingual, duplicated, and prompt-like inputs, with explicit reject behavior and slice deltas.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Maintain a living threat- and shift-based stress matrix, quantify degradation, define safe fallback and monitoring triggers, and update tests after incidents.

examples.title: Robustness, drift and adversarial cases: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: After a source/version change, a monitoring trigger detects calibration drift and routes decisions to review while retraining evidence is gathered.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: After a source/version change, a monitoring trigger detects calibration drift and routes decisions to review while retraining evidence is gathered.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: No finite test proves robustness, adversaries adapt, synthetic perturbations may be unrealistic, drift labels arrive late, monitoring can miss silent harms, and recovery controls need exercises.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: Passing a standard adversarial test suite certifies robustness against future shifts and attacks.

Keep this boundary in view: No finite test proves robustness, adversaries adapt, synthetic perturbations may be unrealistic, drift labels arrive late, monitoring can miss silent harms, and recovery controls need exercises.
